title: India and Germany Sign Multiple Agreements to Deepen Energy and Sustainability Cooperation
content: India and Germany on Monday signed a number of agreements spanning sectors such as defence, technology, health, energy and human resources. Energy and sustainability featured prominently in the outcomes. An offtake agreement was signed between Indiaâ€™s AM Green and Germanyâ€™s Uniper Global Commodities for green ammonia. In addition, a memorandum of understanding (MoU) was also inked between Indiaâ€™s Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board and the German Technical and Scientific Association for Gas and Water Industries (DVGW) in the field of renewable energy. The announcement was made for launch of Battery Storage working group under the India-Germany Platform for Investments in Renewable Energy Worldwide. Germany committed a new funding of 1.24 billion euros under the Green and Sustainable Development Partnership (GSDP) for projects in renewable energy, green hydrogen, the PM e-Bus Sewa programme and climate-resilient urban infrastructure. Germany had earlier committed 10 billion euros under the programme until 2030, largely in the form of concessional loans. Of this, around 5 billion euros have already been utilised or earmarked for projects since 2022, the Prime Minister's Office (PMO) said.
author: Analysts Account
